Off Calendar Jan-28-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: (All appearances by Court call)
Ansel Kinney, 415.331.1410 The Law Offices of Ansel Kinney for Plaintiffs Martin and Claire Ryan.
Jeffrey M. Judd, 415.227.3548 of Buchalter, APC for Plaintiffs Martin and Claire Ryan.
Jason E. Fellner, 415.788.1900 of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney for Defendants/Cross-Complainants 1001 California Street Condominium Association, Property Management One and Karen Katz.
Lindsey A. Morgan, 925. 837.2317 of the Shannon B. Jones Law Group, Inc. for
Defendants/Cross-Complainants, Pacific Union International Inc. & Joanne Stein.
Reporter: Shelia Pham,CSR# 13293, 415.517.5439, spham28@yahoo.com.

PLAINTIFF MARTIN RYAN, CLAIRE RYAN Plaintiffs Claire And Martin Ryans Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Their Motion For Ccp Section 473(B) Relief

Argued. Jason Fellner counsel for Defendant 101 California Street Condominium Association argues that Judge Quidachay should hear this motion and objects to Judge Haines ruling on the matter.

The Court rules as follows: The Honorable Ronald E. Quidachay is not available to hear this Motion. Motion for Reconsideration of Order denying CCP 473(b) motion is granted. Demurrer to the Fourth Amended Complaint is re-set for hearing for February 3, 2021. Plaintiff shall file and serve its oppositions to the demurrers no later than January 27, 2021 by 5:00 p.m. Oppositions shall address solely the issues raised in the demurrers as if filed at the time when opposition was originally due. Oppositions shall be accompanied by meet and confer declarations addressing good faith participation in meet and confer efforts mandated by CCP 430.41. By no later than February 1, 2021 defendants may file their respective replies to Plaintiff's oppositions. Plaintiff's counsel shall remit to defendants their attorney's fees incurred in connection with the demurrers and all filing related to CCP 473(b), Motions for Reconsideration, ex-parte appearance(s), etc. within 30 days. Defendants complied with the Courts instructions to submit this morning by 8:30 a.m. declarations detailing attorney's fees described above. Plaintiffs are ordered to pay counsel for Defendants 1001 California Street Condominium, Property Management One and Karen Katz, $49,206.00 in fees and $750.00 in costs and counsel for Defendants Pacific Union International Inc. and Joanne Stein $48,339.74. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Shelia Pham,CSR# 13293, 415.517.5439, spham28@yahoo.com.(D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For A Terminating Sanction, Or In The Alternative, Issue And Or Evidence Sanctions Against Plaintiffs, And For A Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiffs And Their Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
Ansel Kinney, Esq., (415) 331-1410, appearing for Claire and Martin Ryan, Plaintiff and
Alston L. Lew, Esq., (415) 788-1900, appearing for 1001 CA St. Condominium Association for Defendant
Lindsey A. Morgan, Esq. (925) 837-2317, appearing for Pacific Union International, Inc and Joanne Stein, Defendant/Cross-Complainant,

ARGUED. Mr. Kinney stated that he contested the tentative ruling, argued that a separate statement was not filed and provided case background. Ms. Morgan provided oral argument in support of her position and questioned if the plaintiff is ordered to respond to the interrogatories.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant 1001 California Street Condominium Homeowners Associations Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication, Of Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-26-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Court Call:
Ansel Kinney, Esq., 415-331-1410, appearing for Claire and Martin Ryan, Plaintiff

Alston Lew, Esq., 415-788-1900, appearing for Condominium Associstion, Defendant

Lindsey Morgan, Esq., 925-837-2317, appearing for Pacific Union, Defendant

Peter Solecki, 951-308-1555, appearing for Diane Wiley, Trustee

Court reporter: Alesia Collins. CSR #7751. Telephone: (800)-367-3376.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). Mr. Kinney and Mr. Lew stated that they constested the tenative ruling.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Motion is treated as a motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the causes of actions for breach of governing documents (5th) and breach of Davis-Sterling Act (6th ) and is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege each element of this cause of action as to the failure to provide alternative heating to the unit upon termination of central heating. Motion for Summary Adjudication of the cause of action for Breach of Fiduciary Duty is GRANTED. There are no facts in dispute concerning this cause of action and Plaintiff cites no authority in the opposition suggesting that defendants are not entitled to a judgement as a matter of law based on the arguments and authorities cited in the moving papers. Motion for Summary Adjudication of the remaining causes of action is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist as to the representations made regarding the roof and regarding business judgment rule. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, reported (D501)

Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Zach D. Webster, esq. (415-673-5600) of Goldstein Gellman Melbostad Harris & McSparran LLP for Plaintiff/Moving party Martin Ryan.
Jason Fellner, esq. (415-788-1900) of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney for Defendant/opposing party 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS.

***Defendant's Motion to Strike Complaint and Demurrer to Complaint heard concurrently.***

ARGUED re: Plaintiff's ability to amend in good faith; contesting "with or without leave to amend." The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED in part and SUSTAINED in part. The DEMURRER for failure to join a necessary party is SUSTAINED with leave to amend so Plaintiff can add Claire Ryan as a necessary party, per Plaintiff's agreement during the meet and confer. The Demurrer to the Fourth Cause of Action is OVERRULED. The Davis-Stirling Act allows for the additional relief of attorney's fees. The DEMURRER as to the Sixth and Seventh Causes of Action is SUSTAINED with leave to amend so Plaintiff can allege sufficient facts to state a fraud claim. Order is signed. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Zach D. Webster, esq. (415-673-5600) of Goldstein Gellman Melbostad Harris & McSparran LLP for Plaintiff/Moving party Martin Ryan.
Jason Fellner, esq. (415-788-1900) of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney for Defendant/opposing party 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS.

***Defendant's Motion to Strike Complaint and Demurrer to Complaint heard concurrently.***

ARGUED re: Plaintiff's ability to amend in good faith; contesting "with or without leave to amend." The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend so Plaintiff can allege exemplary damages via a sufficiently alleged fraud claim. Order is signed. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
